_notes: Active-learning ensemble-member learning config, adapting PANAMA's
to train each of the 4 disposable ConcatLSTM members serially on one device.
DEVIATIONS from PANAMA (intentional, for this single-GPU port):
* trainer.accelerator is rewritten at runtime to cuda > mps > cpu, so this stays device-agnostic
* PANAMA disables in-loop validation (check_val_every_n_epoch 51) and takes the last checkpoint.
Our train_ensemble selects the val_loss-monitored BEST checkpoint, so validation must run:
check_val_every_n_epoch is left at its default (1) and the data config keeps a validation split.
* batch_size is 128, not PANAMA's 512. ConcatLSTM trains with full backprop-through-time over each
ny-length window (train_truncate null), so peak memory scales with batch_size * ny. The starter
windows are ny=32768 (above the loss mask_first, below one LSTM block; see the data config), and
512 * 32768 backprop OOMs a Mac (~80 GiB); batch_size 32 peaks near 5 GiB on MPS and a batch_size
of 128 peaks near 20GB. Raise it on a large-VRAM CUDA box; lower it (and/or set drop_last false)
for a very small capture set. Watch the tensorboard to verify that training is converging if you
Plain shuffled DataLoader (no capture-grouped batch sampler) is used.
